title: Deep Learning
description: Learn neural networks through practical architecture and training intuition.
outcomes: Explain layers, weights, and biases in plain language.
Use activation and loss functions to shape model behavior.
Build and evaluate a first small deep-learning model.
Reason about image-classification workflows and limitations.

curatedResources.title: 3Blue1Brown Neural Networks / Deep Learning Visual Series
curatedResources.placement: Use for visual intuition across layers, activations, and training flow.
curatedResources.why: The visuals make core deep learning ideas more intuitive before implementation.
curatedResources.whenToUse: Use alongside early model architecture lessons.

curatedResources.title: Neural Networks: Zero to Hero
curatedResources.why: Strong implementation-focused sequence for understanding deep networks from first principles.
curatedResources.whenToUse: Use after completing this module if you want to build end-to-end from scratch.

curatedResources.title: AI Engineering Hub
curatedResources.placement: Explore applied project examples after your deep-learning portfolio mission.
curatedResources.why: Provides practical implementations you can compare against your own model work.
curatedResources.whenToUse: Use when planning your next portfolio build after core deep-learning concepts.

lessons.title: What is a Neural Network?
lessons.description: Build intuition for neurons, connections, and learnable parameters.
checklist: I can explain what a neuron does in one sentence.
I can map one network layer to an input transformation.
I can describe one reason deep models improve expressiveness.
questions: How would you explain a layer to a non-technical teammate?
Why might depth matter even if it is harder to explain?

curatedResources.title: Neural Networks: Zero to Hero
curatedResources.why: Strengthens your intuition and practical understanding of layers and network behavior.
curatedResources.whenToUse: Use when you want to build an implementation-focused mental model.

lessons.title: Layers and Weights
lessons.description: Understand parameter flow and output shaping across layers.
checklist: I can identify weights as adjustable knobs.
I can explain shape changes across layers.
I can describe one practical architecture reason.
questions: What are weights learning?
How do dimensions affect compatibility between layers?

lessons.title: Activation Functions
lessons.description: Learn why activations allow non-linear learning.
checklist: I can explain why linear-only models are limited.
I can compare two activation tradeoffs.
I can choose an activation for hidden layers.
questions: Why do non-linear activations matter?
When would a saturated activation be harmful?

lessons.title: Loss Functions
lessons.description: Learn how models measure error and learning direction.
checklist: I can define loss as feedback for correction.
I can choose a loss linked to task type.
I can interpret loss curves in training.
questions: How does a loss signal guide optimization?
Why does task type influence the loss choice?

lessons.title: Backpropagation
lessons.description: Understand how errors flow backward to update parameters.
checklist: I can describe gradient flow at a high level.
I can explain when learning signals weaken.
I can suggest one debugging signal in training.
questions: What is the role of gradients in learning?
How do you detect vanishing signal problems early?

curatedResources.title: Neural Networks: Zero to Hero
curatedResources.placement: Use before finalizing optimizer and stabilization choices.
curatedResources.why: Builds practical intuition around gradient flow and training dynamics.
curatedResources.whenToUse: Use when diagnosing unstable learning curves.

lessons.title: Optimizers
lessons.description: Compare optimization strategies for faster and stable convergence.
checklist: I can compare at least two optimizers.
I can explain learning-rate sensitivity.
I can apply one rule for stability.
questions: How can learning rate choices hurt model progress?
When would adaptive optimizers be useful?

lessons.title: PyTorch Basics
lessons.description: Create your first tensor, model, loss, and training loop.
checklist: I can define tensor and model objects.
I can run a tiny forward/backward loop.
I can inspect outputs and debug shapes.
questions: What is your first PyTorch sanity check before training?
Why is deterministic seeding useful in experiments?

lessons.title: Image Classification
lessons.description: Apply deep learning to a small multi-class visual task.
checklist: I can explain input normalization decisions.
I can evaluate class-level quality.
I can describe augmentation as generalization support.
questions: Why do input shapes matter for image models?
What evaluation metric change when classes are imbalanced?

lessons.title: Mini Project: Small CNN Smoke Test
lessons.description: Build a tiny image classifier smoke test with stable logging.
checklist: I can run a small end-to-end training script.
I can show training and validation metric drift.
I can document at least one model limitation.
questions: How will you know when your model memorizes too much?
What is one practical guard against silent failure?

lessons.title: Portfolio Project: Image Classifier
lessons.description: Build a complete image-classification portfolio project.
checklist: I can provide reproducible preprocessing.
I can evaluate per-class performance.
I can explain why architecture choices align with data.
questions: How would you choose model capacity for this dataset?
What would you monitor after launch?

lessons.title: Deep Learning Quiz
lessons.description: Prove readiness for model-level LLM and deployment content.
checklist: I can explain neural forward and backward flow.
I can describe the role of activations and loss.
I can defend my optimizer and architecture choices.
questions: What concept links architecture depth to expressive power?
Why are optimization settings inseparable from model quality?

quizQuestions.prompt: In your own words, explain what a neural network does and why layers help.
quizQuestions.correctAnswer: A neural network transforms inputs through multiple weighted layers, allowing it to learn hierarchical patterns that simple linear models cannot represent.

options.label: Layered weighted pattern learning
options.explanation: A neural network transforms inputs through multiple weighted layers, allowing it to learn hierarchical patterns that simple linear models cannot represent.

options.label: More layers first
options.explanation: More layers cannot fix unclear targets, poor data, or weak evaluation.

options.label: Linear transformations only
options.explanation: Without non-linear activations, deep networks cannot learn rich non-linear patterns.

options.label: Loss as vanity metric
options.explanation: Loss drives training updates and helps diagnose whether learning is improving.

quizQuestions.prompt: What are weights and why are they central during training?
quizQuestions.correctAnswer: Weights are parameters that the model adjusts to reduce error, and they encode learned patterns linking inputs to outputs.

options.label: Linear transformations only
options.explanation: Without non-linear activations, deep networks cannot learn rich non-linear patterns.

options.label: Loss as vanity metric
options.explanation: Loss drives training updates and helps diagnose whether learning is improving.

options.label: Skip reproducibility
options.explanation: Without reproducibility practices, experiments become difficult to compare or debug.

options.label: Learned adjustable parameters
options.explanation: Weights are parameters that the model adjusts to reduce error, and they encode learned patterns linking inputs to outputs.

quizQuestions.prompt: Why are non-linear activations essential in deep learning?
quizQuestions.correctAnswer: Without non-linear activations, stacked layers collapse to a single linear transformation and lose the ability to model complex relationships.

options.label: Loss as vanity metric
options.explanation: Loss drives training updates and helps diagnose whether learning is improving.

options.label: Skip reproducibility
options.explanation: Without reproducibility practices, experiments become difficult to compare or debug.

options.label: Non-linear pattern learning
options.explanation: Without non-linear activations, stacked layers collapse to a single linear transformation and lose the ability to model complex relationships.

options.label: Trust sample predictions
options.explanation: Image models can fail on class imbalance, artifacts, lighting, leakage, and unseen conditions.

quizQuestions.prompt: How does loss connect to model improvement?
quizQuestions.correctAnswer: Loss quantifies prediction error; lowering loss signals the model is improving toward desired targets.

options.label: Skip reproducibility
options.explanation: Without reproducibility practices, experiments become difficult to compare or debug.

options.label: Prediction error signal
options.explanation: Loss quantifies prediction error; lowering loss signals the model is improving toward desired targets.

options.label: Trust sample predictions
options.explanation: Image models can fail on class imbalance, artifacts, lighting, leakage, and unseen conditions.

options.label: Optimizer as guarantee
options.explanation: Optimizers help update weights, but data, architecture, learning rate, and evaluation still matter.

quizQuestions.prompt: Describe backpropagation using a simple analogy.
quizQuestions.correctAnswer: Backpropagation is like tracing blame backward through steps so each weight receives a correction signal based on final output error.

options.label: Trust sample predictions
options.explanation: Image models can fail on class imbalance, artifacts, lighting, leakage, and unseen conditions.

options.label: Optimizer as guarantee
options.explanation: Optimizers help update weights, but data, architecture, learning rate, and evaluation still matter.

options.label: Backward credit assignment
options.explanation: Backpropagation is like tracing blame backward through steps so each weight receives a correction signal based on final output error.

options.label: More layers first
options.explanation: More layers cannot fix unclear targets, poor data, or weak evaluation.

quizQuestions.prompt: How does an optimizer affect training quality?
quizQuestions.correctAnswer: The optimizer controls how aggressively weights are updated from gradients, influencing stability, convergence speed, and final model performance.

options.label: Optimizer as guarantee
options.explanation: Optimizers help update weights, but data, architecture, learning rate, and evaluation still matter.

options.label: More layers first
options.explanation: More layers cannot fix unclear targets, poor data, or weak evaluation.

options.label: Linear transformations only
options.explanation: Without non-linear activations, deep networks cannot learn rich non-linear patterns.

options.label: Gradient update control
options.explanation: The optimizer controls how aggressively weights are updated from gradients, influencing stability, convergence speed, and final model performance.

quizQuestions.prompt: Why are reproducibility practices critical in PyTorch experimentation?
quizQuestions.correctAnswer: Reproducibility lets teams compare runs fairly, debug regressions, and trust that improvements come from design changes.

options.label: More layers first
options.explanation: More layers cannot fix unclear targets, poor data, or weak evaluation.

options.label: Reproducible experiment comparison
options.explanation: Reproducibility lets teams compare runs fairly, debug regressions, and trust that improvements come from design changes.

options.label: Linear transformations only
options.explanation: Without non-linear activations, deep networks cannot learn rich non-linear patterns.

options.label: Loss as vanity metric
options.explanation: Loss drives training updates and helps diagnose whether learning is improving.

quizQuestions.prompt: What are two critical checks before trusting an image classifier in a portfolio
quizQuestions.correctAnswer: Validate class-level performance and inspect confusion patterns, then test on realistic input distributions to avoid accidental overconfidence.

options.label: Class-level and real-world checks
options.explanation: Validate class-level performance and inspect confusion patterns, then test on realistic input distributions to avoid accidental overconfidence.
